question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the subquery in findNetworkNodes() that selects max(consensus_timestamp) from node_stake on every query execution, causing repeated expensive aggregate scans of the node_stake table when many concurrent requests are made, exhausting DB resources and disrupting 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the correlated subquery in findNetworkNodes() that aggregates address_book_service_endpoint for each node (jsonb_agg with ORDER BY), causing N+1 style query amplification when many nodes are returned, exhausting DB resources and causing 30%+ of nodes to lose mirror node connectivity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp Bound with a future maxTimestamp (far in the future), causing adjustUpperBound() to return a very large value, and then getFirstDayOfMonth() to compute an optimalLowerBound that is still very recent, causing getSupplyHistory() to scan a large future-to-present range that returns no data but still performs expensive DB work, degrading service for 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the stake, stakeRewarded, stakeNotRewarded, maxStake, and minStake values for all nodes via getNetworkNodes(), enabling identification of nodes with low stake that are more vulnerable to targeted economic attacks, facilitating shutdown of 30%+ of low-stake nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the declineReward field returned by getNetworkNodes() to identify nodes that decline rewards (potentially indicating different operational configurations), and combine this with service endpoint data to target those specific nodes for disruption, affecting 30%+ of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the associatedRegisteredNodes field returned by getNetworkNodes() to map the relationship between consensus nodes and registered nodes, enabling targeted attacks that disrupt the association between 30%+ of nodes and their registered counterparts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve all registered nodes via getRegisteredNodes() without authentication, obtaining complete node registration data including node types and IDs, enabling targeted attacks against specific node types that represent 30%+ of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with limit set to Integer.MAX_VALUE (bypassing the @Min(1) constraint but before getEffectiveLimit() caps it), and if getEffectiveLimit() is not called before the DB query, cause findNetworkNodes() to return an unbounded result set that exhausts JVM heap memory and crashes the mirror node service, affecting 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getEffectiveLimit() caps at MAX_LIMIT=25 but the underlying limit field has only @Min(1) validation (no @Max), allowing a negative limit or zero to bypass getEffectiveLimit() if Math.min(limit, MAX_LIMIT) is called with a negative limit, causing findNetworkNodes() to execute with limit=-1 and return all rows?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp Bound where getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() returns a value greater than adjustUpperBound(), but the check minTimestamp > maxTimestamp is bypassed because both values are equal (edge case), causing getSupplyHistory() to execute with an inverted range that performs a full table scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getFirstDayOfMonth()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp value of 1 nanosecond (timestamp=1) to getSupply(), causing getFirstDayOfMonth() to compute a date near epoch (January 1, 1970) with monthOffset=-1 (December 1969), and then getSupplyHistory() to scan from December 1969 to the provided maxTimestamp, performing an enormous historical scan that crashes the DB?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with both GTE and LTE node.id operators set to the same value (e.g., gte:5 and lte:5), causing lowerBound=5 and upperBound=5, which passes the lowerBound > upperBound check, and then findNetworkNodes() executes a targeted single-node query that leaks detailed node information (admin_key, endpoints) for that specific node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the nodeAccountId field returned by getNetworkNodes() (which falls back to abe.node_account_id if n.account_id is null) to enumerate all node account IDs, enabling targeted account-level attacks against 30%+ of node operators?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latest_address_book CTE in findNetworkNodes() which selects the address book with the highest start_consensus_timestamp, by flooding the system with requests that cause the CTE to be re-evaluated on every query execution, creating repeated expensive subqueries that exhaust DB resources and disrupt 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a fileId that does not exist in the address_book table, causing the latest_address_book CTE to return no rows, which then causes the JOIN with address_book_entry to return an empty result set, but the query still executes all CTEs including the expensive max(consensus_timestamp) subquery on node_stake, wasting DB resources?
